verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth Me: and He that receiveth Me, receiveth Him that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Great is the recompense of care bestowed upon the servants of God, and of itself it yieldeth to us its fruits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “he that receiveth you,” it saith, “receiveth Me, and he that receiveth Me, receiveth Him that sent Me.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now what can be equal to the receiving Christ and His Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what kind of connection hath this with what was said before?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What hath it in common with that which He had said, “If ye do these things happy are ye,” to add, “He that receiveth you”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A close connection, and very harmonious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they were about to go forth and to suffer many dreadful things, He comforteth them in two ways; one derived from Himself, the other derived from others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if,” He saith, “ye are truly wise, ever keeping Me in mind, and bearing about all both what I said, and what I did, ye will easily endure terrible things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not in this way only, but also from your enjoying great attention from all men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The first point He declared when He said, “If ye do these things happy are ye”; the second when He said, “He that receiveth you receiveth Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He opened the houses of all men to them, so that both from the sound wisdom of their manners, and the zeal of those who would tend them, they might have twofold comfort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when He had given these directions to them as to men about to run through all the world, reflecting that the traitor was deprived of both of these things, and would enjoy neither of them, neither patience in toils, nor the service of kind entertainers, He again was troubled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Evangelist to signify this besides, and to show that it was on his account that He was troubled, adds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When Jesus had thus said, He was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He bringeth fear on all by not mentioning (the traitor) by name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But they are in doubt”; although conscious to themselves of nothing evil; but they deemed the declaration of Christ more to be believed than their own thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they “looked one on another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By laying the whole upon one, Jesus would have cut short their fear, but by adding, “one of you,” He troubled all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The rest looked upon one another; but the ever fervent Peter “beckoneth” to John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since he had been before rebuked, and when Christ desired to wash him would have hindered Him, and since he is everywhere found moved indeed by love, yet blamed; being on this account afraid, he neither kept quiet, nor did he speak, but wished to gain information by means of John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is a question worth asking, why when all were distressed, and trembling, when their leader was afraid, John like one at ease leans on Jesus’ bosom, and not only leans, but even (lies) on His breast?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor is this the only thing worthy of enquiry, but that also which follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What he saith of himself, “Whom Jesus loved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did no one else say this of himself? yet the others were loved too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he more than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if no other hath said this about him, but he about himself, it is nothing wonderful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Paul too does the same, when occasion calls, saying thus, “I knew a man fourteen years ago”; yet in fact he has gone through other no trifling praises of himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seems it to thee a small thing that, when he had heard, “Follow Me,” he straightway left his nets, and his father, and followed; and that Christ took him alone with Peter into the mountain, ( Matt. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ,) and another time again when He went into a house? ( Luke viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What high praise also has he himself passed on Peter without concealment, telling us that Christ said, “Peter, lovest thou Me more than these?” ( c. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ), and everywhere he showeth him warm, and nobly disposed towards himself; for instance, when he said, “Lord, and what shall this man do?” he spake from great love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did no other say (this) concerning him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because he would not himself have said it, unless he had come to this passage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if after telling us that Peter beckoned to John to ask, he had added nothing more, he would have caused considerable doubt, and have compelled us to enquire into the reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order therefore himself to solve this difficulty, he saith, “He lay on the bosom of Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thinkest thou that thou hast learnt a little thing when thou hast heard that “he lay,” and that their Master allowed such boldness to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou desirest to know the cause of this, the action was of love; wherefore he saith, “Whom Jesus loved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I suppose also that John doth this for another reason, as wishing to show that he was exempt from the charge and so he speaks openly and is confident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, why did he use these words, not at any other point of time, but only when the chief of the Apostles beckoned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mightest not deem that Peter beckoned to him as being greater, he saith that the thing took place because of the great love (which Jesus bare him).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why doth he even lie on His bosom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had not as yet formed any high surmises concerning Him; besides, in this way He calmed their despondency; for it is probable that at this time their faces were overclouded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If they were troubled in their souls, much more would they be so in their countenances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Soothing them therefore by word and by the question, He makes a way beforehand, and allows him to lean on His breast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe too his modesty; he mentions not his own name, but, “whom He loved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As also Paul, when he said, “I knew a man about fourteen years ago.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now for the first time Jesus convicted the traitor, but not even now by name; but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He it is, to whom I shall give a sop when I have dipped it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even the manner (of the rebuke) was calculated to put him to shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He respected not the table, though he shared the bread; be it so; but the receiving the sop from His own hand, whom would not that have won over? yet him it won not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then Satan entered into him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Laughing at him for his shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As long as he belonged to the band of disciples he dared not spring upon him, but attacked him from without; but when Christ made him manifest and separated him, then he sprang upon him without fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was not fitting to keep within one of such a character, and who so long had remained incorrigible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He henceforth cast him out, and then that other seized him when cut off, and he leaving them went forth by night.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto him, Friend, that thou doest, do quickly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now no man at the table knew with what intent He spake this unto him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wonderful insensibility!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How could it be that he was neither softened nor shamed; but rendered yet more shameless, “went out.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The “do quickly,” is not the expression of one commanding, nor advising, but of one reproaching, and showing him that He desired to correct him, but that since he was incorrigible, He let him go.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this, the Evangelist saith, “no man of those that sat at the table knew.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some one may perhaps find here a considerable difficulty, if, when the disciples had asked, “Who is it?” and He had answered, “He to whom I shall give a sop when I have dipped it,” they did not even so understand; unless indeed He spake it secretly, so that no man should hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For John on this very account, leaning by His breast, asked Him almost close to His ear, so that the traitor might not be made manifest; and Christ answered in like manner, so that not even then did He discover him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And though He spake emphatically, “Friend, that thou doest, do quickly,” even so they understood not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he spake thus to show that the things were true which had been said by Him to the Jews concerning His death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He had said to them, “I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again”: and, “No man taketh it from Me.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As long then as He would retain it, no man was able (to take it); but when He resigned it, then the action became easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All this He implied when He said, “That thou doest, do quickly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even then did He expose him, for perhaps the others might have torn him in pieces, or Peter might have killed him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account “no man at the table knew.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not even John?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not even he: for he could not have expected that a disciple would arrive at such a pitch of wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they were far from such iniquity themselves, they could not suspect such things concerning others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As before He had told them, “I speak not of you all” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ), yet did not reveal the person; so here, they thought that it was said concerning some other matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It was night,” saith the Evangelist, when he went out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why tellest thou me the time?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn his forwardness, that not even the time restrained him from his purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even did this make him quite manifest, for the others were at this time in confusion, occupied by fear and great distress, and they knew not the true reason of what had been said but supposed that He spake thus, in order that Judas might give somewhat to the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He cared greatly for the poor, teaching us also to bestow much diligence on this thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they thought this, not without a cause, but “because he had the bag.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet no one appears to have brought money to Him; that the female disciples nourished Him of their substance, it has said, but this it hath nowhere intimated. ( Luke viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how did He who bade His disciples bear neither scrip, nor money, nor staff, Himself bear a bag to minister to the poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn, that it behooveth even him who is exceedingly needy and crucified, to be very careful on this point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For many things He did in the way of dispensation for our instruction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The disciples then thought that He said this, that Judas should give something to the poor; and not even this shamed him, His not being willing even to the last day to make him a public example.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We too ought to do the like, and not parade the sins of our companions, though they be incurable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even after this He gave a kiss to the man who came to betray Him, and endured, such an action as that was, and then proceeded to a thing of far greater daring, the Cross itself, to the death of shame, and there again He manifested His lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here He calleth it “glory,” showing us that there is nothing so shameful and reproachful which makes not brighter him who goeth to it, if it be done according to the will of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least after the going forth of Judas to the betraying, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now is the Son of Man glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this way rousing the dejected thoughts of the disciples, and persuading them not only not to despond, but even to rejoice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He rebuked Peter at the first, because for one who has been in death to overcome death, is great glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is what He said of Himself, “When I am lifted up, then ye shall know that I Am” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 28 ); and again, “Destroy this Temple” ( c. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ); and again, “No sign shall be given unto you but the sign of Jonas.” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how can it be otherwise than great glory, the being able even after death to do greater things than before death? for in order that the Resurrection might be believed, the disciples did work greater things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But unless He had lived, and had been God, how could these men have wrought such things in His Name?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And God shall glorify Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “And God shall glorify Him in Himself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is “by means of Himself, not by means of another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And shall straightway glorify Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “simultaneously with the Cross.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For it will not be after much time,” He saith, “nor will He wait for the distant season of the Resurrection, nor will He then show Him glorious, but straightway on the Cross itself His glories shall appear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so the sun was darkened, the rocks rent; the veil of the temple was parted asunder, many bodies of saints that slept arose, the tomb had its seals, the guards sat by, and while a stone lay over the Body the Body rose; forty days passed by, and the Gift of the Spirit came, and they all straightway preached Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is, “shall glorify Him in Himself, and shall straightway glorify Him”; not by Angels or Archangels, not by any other power, but by Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how did He also glorify Him by Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By doing all for the glory of the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Son did all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He referreth to the Father the things done by Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Little children, yet a little while I am with you—and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go ye cannot come, so now I say to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He now begins words of sorrow after the supper.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Judas went forth it was no longer evening, but night.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since they were about to come shortly, it was necessary to set all things before the disciples, that they might have them in remembrance; or rather, the Spirit recalled all to their minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is likely that they would forget many things, as hearing for the first time, and being about to undergo such temptations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Men who were weighed down to sleep, (as another Evangelist saith,— Luke xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 45 ,) who were possessed by despondency, as Christ saith Himself, “Because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your hearts” ( c. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), how could they retain all these things exactly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then were they spoken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It became no little gain to them with respect to their opinion of Christ, that in after times when reminded they certainly knew that they had long ago heard these things from Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He first cast down their souls, saying, “Yet a little while I am with you”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “To the Jews indeed it was said with reason, but wherefore dost Thou place us in just the same class with those obstinate ones?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He by no means did so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then said He, ‘As I said to the Jews’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He reminded them that He did not now, because troubles were upon them, warn them of these things, but that He had foreknown them from the first, and that they were witnesses who had heard that He had said these things to the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He added also the word, “little children,” that when they heard, “As I said to the Jews,” they might not deem that the expression was used in like sense towards themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was not then to depress but to comfort them that He thus spake, that their dangers might not, by coming upon them suddenly, trouble them to excess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whither I go, ye cannot come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that His death is a removal, and a change for the better to a place which admits not corruptible bodies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He saith, both to excite their love towards Him, and to make it more fervent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye know that when we see any of our dearest friends departing from us, our affection is warmest, and the more so, when we see them going to a place to which it is not even possible for us to go.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things then He said, terrifying the Jews, but kindling longing in the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Such is the place, that not only not they, but not even you, My best beloved, can come there.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He showeth also His Own dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So now I say to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In one way to them, to you in another way”; that is, “not with them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when did the Jews seek Him, when the disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The disciples, when they fled the Jews, when they suffered miseries unendurable and surpassing all description at the capture of their city, when the wrath of God was borne down upon them from every side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To the Jews therefore He spake then, because of their unbelief, “but to you now, that troubles might not come upon you unexpected.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A new commandment I give unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since it was likely that they would be troubled when they heard these things, as though they were about to be deserted, He comforteth them, investing them with that which was the root of all blessings and a safeguard, love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Grieve ye at My departure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, if ye love one another, ye shall be the stronger.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then said He not this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He said what profited them more than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “By this shall all men know that ye are My disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this He at the same time showed that the company should never be extinguished, when He gave them a distinguishing token.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said when the traitor was cut off from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how calleth He that a new commandment which is contained also in the Old (covenant)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He made it new Himself by the manner; therefore He added, “As I have loved you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have not paid back to you a debt of good deeds first done by you, but Myself have begun,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And so ought you to benefit your dearest ones, though you owe them nothing”; and omitting to speak of the miracles which they should do, He maketh their characteristic, love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it is this which chiefly shows men holy; it is the foundation of all virtue; by this mostly we are all even saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “this,” He saith, “is to be a disciple; so shall all men praise you, when they see you imitating My Love.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not miracles much more show this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “many will say, Lord, have we not in Thy Name cast out devils?” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, when they rejoice that the devils obey them, He saith, “Rejoice not that the devils obey you, but that your names are written in heaven.” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this indeed brought over the world, because that was before it; had not that been, neither would this have endured.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then straightway made them perfect, the having all one heart and one soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But had they separated one from the other, all things would have been lost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now He spake this not to them only, but to all who should believe on Him; since even now, there is nothing else that causes the heathen to stumble, except that there is no love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “they also urge against us the absence of miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But not in the same way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But where did the Apostles manifest their love?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou Peter and John inseparable from one another, and going up to the Temple? ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou Paul disposed in a like way towards them, and dost thou doubt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If they had gained the other blessings, much more had they the mother of them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is a thing that springs from a virtuous soul; but where wickedness is, there the plant withers away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “when,” it saith, “iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.” ( Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And miracles do not so much attract the heathen as the mode of life; and nothing so much causes a right life as love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those who wrought miracles they often even called deceivers; but they could have no hold upon a pure life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While then the message of the Gospel was not yet spread abroad, miracles were with good reason marveled at, but now men must get to be admired by their lives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing so raises respect in the heathen as virtue, nothing so offends them as vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When one of them sees the greedy man, the plunderer, exhorting others to do the contrary, when he sees the man who was commanded to love even his enemies, treating his very kindred like brutes, he will say that the words are folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he sees one trembling at death, how will he receive the accounts of immortality?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he sees us fond of rule, and slaves to the other passions, he will more firmly remain in his own doctrines, forming no high opinion of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We, we are the cause of their remaining in their error.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Their own doctrines they have long condemned, and in like manner they admire ours, but they are hindered by our mode of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To follow wisdom in talk is easy, many among themselves have done this; but they require the proof by works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then let them look to the ancients of our profession.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But about them they by no means believe; they enquire concerning those now living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “show me,” it saith, “thy faith by thy works” ( Jas. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ); but this is not the case; on the contrary, seeing us tear our neighbors worse than any wild beast, they call us the curse of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things restrain the heathen, and suffer them not to come over to our side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that we shall be punished for these also; not only for what we do amiss ourselves, but because the name of God is blasphemed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we be given up to wealth, and luxury, and the other passions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the future let us leave them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what the Prophet saith of certain foolish ones, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we die.” ( Isa. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in the present case we cannot even say this, so “many” gather round themselves what belongs to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So chiding them also, the Prophet said, “Will ye dwell alone upon the earth?” ( Isa. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I fear lest some grievous thing come to pass, and we draw down upon us heavy vengeance from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that this may not come to pass, let us be careful of all virtue, that we may obtain the future blessings, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory now and forever, and world without end.
